notes: Investment balances can never go negative. After a thorough propagation audit across every input surface (rates, salary, appraisal month, expenses, growth rates, cash rate, investment contribution/rate, EMI amount/close, one-offs, account balances, transaction edits, instrument deletion, horizon changes), the engine wiring was confirmed sound — all input→page paths propagate correctly. The one confirmed bug: editing a CLOSED investment's monthly contribution (or other edge cases) could drive its projected balance negative. Fixed by clamping investment balances to >= 0 in both the main projection loop and the annual-compounding branch. KNOWN ITEM (deliberately deferred to its own careful build): current-month savings forecast uses the engine's PLANNED surplus, so logging a large current-month expense does not yet re-forecast future savings (next month's plan does reflect it). This touches the savings chain (projectSavingsChain) and needs dedicated double-count verification before changing. Reusable SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js are included to re-run all invariants from the console anytime.
